theme_notes: Trump formally declared the US-Iran ceasefire 'OVER' as both sides traded strikes over Strait of Hormuz access, with JD Vance and Jared Kushner heading to Oman for talks; Spain beat Belgium with a late goal to set up a World Cup semifinal vs France in Arlington on July 14; Martha Lillard, last US iron lung polio patient, died at 78; the Big Boy 1940s steam locomotive tours the country for America's 250th anniversary.
settled: You're square. Like the Strait of Hormuz ceasefire — enjoy it while it lasts.
Settled. Unlike every other international situation this week.
Balance nearly zero. Spain plays France in three days. Root for the balance instead.
youOwe: Under fifty bucks. Less than a World Cup hot dog in Dallas. Still embarrassing.
Smaller than Trump's last ceasefire. Lasts about as long too.
Less than a week of iron lung electricity. Cough it up.
Spain needed a late winner to get past Belgium. You need a late payment plan.
Not enough to control the Strait of Hormuz. Enough to ruin a Wednesday.
Could've been a Big Boy locomotive souvenir. Instead it's this.
World Cup nosebleed seat money. You're not even in the stadium.
Iran attacked ships in international waters. You attacked a menu.
Martha Lillard survived in an iron lung for 70 years. You can't survive one tab.
Semifinal seats in Arlington. You chose this debt instead.
Your spending has been formally declared 'OVER' by everyone except you.
The kind of number where countries send mediators. Or at least JD Vance.
America turns 250 this year. This debt feels 250 years old.
The US launched 90 airstrikes and still doesn't run the Strait. Same energy.
This isn't a tab anymore. This is a geopolitical crisis.
The 2026 World Cup trophy costs less. You're not even competing.
Trump called the ceasefire 'OVER.' Your partner's goodwill followed.
You owe more than a Hormuz transit fee. For a fleet. Both directions.
theyOwe: They owe you less than a World Cup foam finger. Still collect it.
Barely qualifies as a Hormuz incident. Still file a claim.
Barely more than a World Cup beer. They should've covered it on the spot.
That's World Cup merch money. They spent it on themselves.
The Big Boy locomotive is touring the country. Your partner is touring excuses.
France plays Spain in three days. You're still waiting for a Venmo.
They said 'I'll get you next time.' That was twelve next times ago.
Ceasefire talks are happening in Oman this weekend. Yours need to happen now.
Semifinal money. They watched the game. You watched the debt compound.
Nearly four figures. Diplomats have convened for less.
JD Vance flew to Oman to fix a dispute this size. You got a shrug.
They swore they'd pay. Iran swore to hold the ceasefire. You see the pattern.
Bring in Jared Kushner. This has become a diplomatic incident.
Iran needed 90 US airstrikes to change their tune. Consider your leverage.
America rebuilt its entire foreign policy this year. Rebuild this ledger.
The Strait of Hormuz is contested territory. So is your savings account.
The US spends less securing Hormuz shipping lanes. Per week.
File a claim with The Hague. Or at minimum, small claims court.
